test_case.question: What is the penalty percentage for a delivery that is 3-5 days late?
test_case.ground_truth: A delivery that is 3-5 days late incurs a penalty of 5% of the order value.

results.answer: Per Section 2. Penalty Structure of tata_steel_contract.md, the penalty is 5% of order value.

chunks.text:
## 2. Penalty Structure

### Late Delivery Penalties
1-2 days late: 2% of order value. 3-5 days late: 5% of order value. 6-10 days late: 10% of order value. More than 10 days late: 15% of order value plus right to cancel.

### Quality Defect Penalties
Defect rate 2-5%: 5% credit on affected shipment. Defect rate 5-10%: 15% credit plus root cause analysis required. Defect rate above 10%: full replacement at vendor cost plus 10% penalty.

## 1. SLA Terms

### Delivery Performance
On-time delivery rate must be minimum 95%. Lead time is 7 business days for standard orders, 3 business days for rush orders. Orders must arrive within plus or minus 1 day of scheduled delivery to qualify as on-time.

### Quality Standards
Maximum 2% defective units per shipment. Steel coil must meet IS 2062 Grade A standards. Certificate of Conformity and Material Test Reports required with each shipment.

## Route Disruption Procedure

When a route status changes to "disrupted," all pending shipments on that route are flagged. The operations team identifies the root cause (weather, infrastructure, labor). If the disruption is weather-related, check force majeure clauses in vendor contracts before assessing penalties.

## Route Selection Rules

Routes are selected based on transit time, cost, and reliability. For urgent shipments (under 5 days lead time), prioritize speed over cost. For standard shipments, use the lowest-cost active route. No single route should exceed 70% utilization.

## Port Congestion Response

When a port status is "congested," notify all shippers within 24 hours. If congestion exceeds 3 days, activate modal shift from sea to rail. If congestion exceeds 7 days, escalate to Supply Chain Director and consider emergency procurement from alternative suppliers.

How is the penalty amount determined in the context of the tata_steel_contract.md?

sentences: A delivery that is 3-5 days late incurs a penalty of 5% of the order value.

test_case.question: What is the maximum storage capacity of the Delhi Hub warehouse?
test_case.ground_truth: The Delhi Hub (warehouse_1) has a maximum storage capacity of 800 units.

results.answer: The maximum capacity of Delhi Hub (warehouse_1) is 800 units (Section: Stock Level Thresholds).

chunks.text:
## Stock Level Thresholds

Each warehouse has defined stock thresholds. Minimum stock level: 200 units. Reorder trigger: 250 units. Maximum capacity: Delhi Hub (warehouse_1) holds 800 units, Bangalore Hub (warehouse_2) holds 600 units. When stock drops below minimum, emergency replenishment is triggered.
# Warehouse Capacity Policy

## Reorder Process

When stock at any warehouse hits the reorder trigger (250 units), an automatic purchase order is generated to the primary vendor. Delhi Hub currently holds 450 units (above threshold). Bangalore Hub currently holds 280 units (near reorder trigger). Reorder quantity is calculated as maximum capacity minus current stock.

## Factory Maintenance Protocol

When a factory enters "maintenance" status, downstream production is halted. Inventory at connected warehouses is assessed. If stock levels fall below minimum threshold (200 units), trigger emergency procurement from backup vendors.

## 3. Delivery Commitments

Primary supply route originates from Jamshedpur Steel Plant in Jharkhand to Mumbai Plant (factory_1) via rail and road multimodal transport. Transit time is 5-7 business days. Backup route goes through Kolkata and activates automatically when primary route transit exceeds 10 days.

sentences: The Delhi Hub (warehouse_1) has a maximum storage capacity of 800 units.

test_case.question: At what stock level is an automatic reorder triggered?
test_case.ground_truth: An automatic purchase order is generated when stock at any warehouse hits the reorder trigger of 250 units.

results.answer: The stock level at which an automatic reorder is triggered is 250 units. (Reorder Process, warehouse_capacity_policy.md)

chunks.text:
## Reorder Process

When stock at any warehouse hits the reorder trigger (250 units), an automatic purchase order is generated to the primary vendor. Delhi Hub currently holds 450 units (above threshold). Bangalore Hub currently holds 280 units (near reorder trigger). Reorder quantity is calculated as maximum capacity minus current stock.

## Stock Level Thresholds

Each warehouse has defined stock thresholds. Minimum stock level: 200 units. Reorder trigger: 250 units. Maximum capacity: Delhi Hub (warehouse_1) holds 800 units, Bangalore Hub (warehouse_2) holds 600 units. When stock drops below minimum, emergency replenishment is triggered.

## Critical Product Priority

Products marked as "critical" priority (Steel Coil, Steel Sheets) receive preferential warehouse allocation. During capacity constraints, standard priority products (Petrochemical Products) are deprioritized. Critical products must maintain a minimum 30-day supply buffer.

## Inventory Reconciliation

Monthly physical inventory counts are mandatory. Variance above 3% triggers an audit. All product movements between warehouses must be logged with timestamps and authorization codes.

## Factory Maintenance Protocol

When a factory enters "maintenance" status, downstream production is halted. Inventory at connected warehouses is assessed. If stock levels fall below minimum threshold (200 units), trigger emergency procurement from backup vendors.

test_case.question: What is the lead time for rush orders under the Tata Steel contract?
test_case.ground_truth: Rush orders have a lead time of 3 business days.

results.answer: Per Section 3. Delivery Commitments of tata_steel_contract.md, the lead time for rush orders is 3 business days.

chunks.text:
## 1. SLA Terms

### Delivery Performance
On-time delivery rate must be minimum 95%. Lead time is 7 business days for standard orders, 3 business days for rush orders. Orders must arrive within plus or minus 1 day of scheduled delivery to qualify as on-time.

### Quality Standards
Maximum 2% defective units per shipment. Steel coil must meet IS 2062 Grade A standards. Certificate of Conformity and Material Test Reports required with each shipment.

# Vendor Contract: Tata Steel

**Contract ID:** VND-2024-001
**Vendor:** Tata Steel
**Material:** Steel Coil
**Effective Date:** January 1, 2024
**Expiration Date:** December 31, 2025

## 3. Delivery Commitments

Primary supply route originates from Jamshedpur Steel Plant in Jharkhand to Mumbai Plant (factory_1) via rail and road multimodal transport. Transit time is 5-7 business days. Backup route goes through Kolkata and activates automatically when primary route transit exceeds 10 days.

## Vendor Notification

All vendors with active shipments on affected routes must be notified within 2 hours of cyclone reaching severe status. Vendors covered under force majeure (see vendor contracts) are exempt from delivery penalties during the cyclone period. Tata Steel (vendor_1) and Reliance Industries (vendor_2) both have force majeure clauses covering natural disasters.

## Port Congestion Response

When a port status is "congested," notify all shippers within 24 hours. If congestion exceeds 3 days, activate modal shift from sea to rail. If congestion exceeds 7 days, escalate to Supply Chain Director and consider emergency procurement from alternative suppliers.

sentences: Rush orders have a lead time of 3 business days.

test_case.question: How quickly must a high severity incident be acknowledged?
test_case.ground_truth: High severity incidents require acknowledgment within 1 hour and an action plan within 4 hours.

results.answer: High severity incidents require acknowledgment within 1 hour, per Section 1 of Response Timelines.

chunks.text:
## Response Timelines

High severity incidents require acknowledgment within 1 hour and an action plan within 4 hours. Medium severity requires acknowledgment within 4 hours and action plan within 24 hours. Low severity is handled in the next business day cycle.

## Incident Classification

Incidents are classified by severity. High severity: complete route or port shutdown, factory offline. Medium severity: partial disruption, reduced capacity, delays under 5 days. Low severity: minor delays, documentation issues.
# Incident Management SOP

## 4. Force Majeure

Events considered force majeure with no penalties applied: natural disasters including cyclones, earthquakes, and floods. Government-imposed restrictions or lockdowns. Labor strikes at vendor facilities with maximum 15 days coverage. Vendor must notify within 24 hours of force majeure event.

## Post-Cyclone Recovery

Within 24 hours of cyclone downgrade, assess port and route infrastructure. Vizag Port must pass safety inspection before resuming full operations. Route_2 requires road condition assessment before reactivation. Expected recovery time: 3-7 days after cyclone passes.

generated_questions: What is the expected response time for high severity incidents according to Response Timelines?

According to Response Timelines, how long is a company expected to take to acknowledge high severity incidents?

sentences: High severity incidents require acknowledgment within 1 hour and an action plan within 4 hours.

test_case.question: What is the backup route if route_1 sea route is disrupted?
test_case.ground_truth: If route_1 (sea) is disrupted, route_3 (rail) is used as the backup.

results.answer: Use route_3 (rail) as a backup. (From Alternative Routing, shipping_policy.md)

chunks.text:
## Alternative Routing

If route_1 (sea) is disrupted, use route_3 (rail) as backup. If route_2 (land) is disrupted, use route_3 (rail) at an 8% cost premium. All rerouting decisions above 25% cost increase require Supply Chain Director approval.
# Shipping Policy: Route Selection and Disruption Protocols

## Route Disruption Procedure

When a route status changes to "disrupted," all pending shipments on that route are flagged. The operations team identifies the root cause (weather, infrastructure, labor). If the disruption is weather-related, check force majeure clauses in vendor contracts before assessing penalties.

## Rerouting During Cyclone

When Cyclone Dana is active, route_2 (Chennai-Delhi land route) is suspended. All cargo originally planned for route_2 is redirected to route_3 (Kolkata-Delhi rail route). Vizag Port operations are reduced to emergency-only berthing. Shipments through Vizag are diverted to Chennai Port (port_2).

## Route Selection Rules

Routes are selected based on transit time, cost, and reliability. For urgent shipments (under 5 days lead time), prioritize speed over cost. For standard shipments, use the lowest-cost active route. No single route should exceed 70% utilization.

sentences: If route_1 (sea) is disrupted, route_3 (rail) is used as the backup.

test_case.question: What is the cost increase when diverting cargo from Vizag Port to Chennai Port?
test_case.ground_truth: Diverting from Vizag Port (port_1) to Chennai Port (port_2) incurs a 12% cost increase.

results.answer: Per Section 1 of shipping_policy.md, when Vizag Port is congested, divert to Chennai Port at a 12% cost increase.

chunks.text:
## Weather Disruption Protocol

When a cyclone or severe weather is detected, any route in the affected zone is marked "disrupted." Cargo in transit is held at the nearest port. New shipments are rerouted to the next available active route. If Vizag Port (port_1) is congested, divert to Chennai Port (port_2) at a 12% cost increase.

## Rerouting During Cyclone

When Cyclone Dana is active, route_2 (Chennai-Delhi land route) is suspended. All cargo originally planned for route_2 is redirected to route_3 (Kolkata-Delhi rail route). Vizag Port operations are reduced to emergency-only berthing. Shipments through Vizag are diverted to Chennai Port (port_2).

## Port Congestion Procedure

When port throughput drops below 60% of capacity, classify as medium severity. Below 30%, classify as high severity. Immediate actions: assess queue depth, identify alternative ports, notify affected vendors. If Vizag Port is congested, priority berthing is negotiated for critical product shipments (Steel Coil, Steel Sheets).

## Port Congestion Response

When a port status is "congested," notify all shippers within 24 hours. If congestion exceeds 3 days, activate modal shift from sea to rail. If congestion exceeds 7 days, escalate to Supply Chain Director and consider emergency procurement from alternative suppliers.

## Alternative Routing

If route_1 (sea) is disrupted, use route_3 (rail) as backup. If route_2 (land) is disrupted, use route_3 (rail) at an 8% cost premium. All rerouting decisions above 25% cost increase require Supply Chain Director approval.

Vizag Port operations are reduced to emergency-only berthing, with shipments diverted to Chennai Port.
